sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 28

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 28:1
items.html:
(to the question) Delet ve Tzir Aleph [a door and one hinge]
In order to understand the discernments of the door [Delet] and the hinge [Tzir] that are made in the Yesod of the Nukva, we must understand well the quality of the Yesod. It is known that at the time of the Ibur [conception], Malchut received the measure of the Yesod, as the Rav wrote on p. 769, Item 52. And you already know that the root of the Yesod was formed in the Yesod of AB of AK, where the Light of Malchut clothed in the vessel of Yesod and was formed there in a discernment of narrow and long [Tzar ve Arich]: narrow in the Light of Hasadim [mercies], because of the refining of the Masach [screen] of Bechina Aleph that took place in the vessel of Hod, so that it remained without Hasadim; but still long in Chochma, for the level of Chochma is called "long," since there it is in the discernment of AB, which is entirely Chochma. Yet even that blocking of Hasadim was of great benefit. For therefore, at the time of Tzimtzum Bet [the second restriction], when the lower Hey was included in each and every Sefira due to the ascent of the lower Hey to the Eynaim [eyes], as is known, the Yesod was not marked by that ascent. This is because the main marking of the lower Hey in the Sefirot — in the secret of the left line in every Sefira, as is known — came about because wherever the lower Hey came, the Light would depart from there; by this power she was marked there and became the left line of that Sefira. Not so the vessel of Yesod: since from its source it stood in a great blocking of Hasadim, the passing of Malchut and the lower Hey through it caused no new action, for it lacked all Light in any case; hence it remained clean of Tzimtzum Bet at that time. Moreover, even at the time of the breaking of the vessels, when the power of Tzimtzum Bet in the Parsa caused the departure of the Light and the death of the kings [Melachim], it did not touch or harm the vessel of Yesod at all, from the aspect of its root mentioned above. And although the vessel of Yesod was also broken together with the rest of the kings, it was not broken entirely, for the aforementioned discernment of Gevura extended there from AB remained alive, as the Rav writes in Etz Chaim, Gate 8, Chapter 4.
